title: Fix Unique Constraint Violation on User Invite Registration
description:
A `UniqueConstraintViolationException` was thrown when a user who was previously invited tried to register. The error was a duplicate entry for the `dtp_users_email_unique` constraint.

### Root Cause
When a user invites another user by email, a temporary account is created without a Salesforce User ID (SFUID). The existing-user check was matching on SFUID rather than email. When the invited user later registered via Salesforce OAuth, the system tried to create a new record with the same email, violating the unique constraint.

### Fix
Updated the existing-user check to match on email address instead of SFUID, so invited users' temp accounts are properly found and updated rather than duplicated.

Detected via New Relic error monitoring.
acceptance_criteria: Invited users can complete registration without a unique constraint error
Existing temp accounts are found by email and updated with Salesforce data
No duplicate user records are created for the same email
middleware_changes: Updated user registration logic to check for existing users by email address instead of SFUID, preventing duplicate entries for invited users.
implementation_notes: The issue occurred because invited users get a temp account with no SFUID. The SFUID-based lookup missed these records. Fixed by checking email first. Detected via New Relic error group monitoring.
comment_summary: Developer explained the root cause: invited users have no SFUID in their temp account, so the SFUID-based lookup failed. Fix was already deployed to staging and queued for production merge.
